metadata.title: Guideline on the quality, non-clinical and clinical aspects of gene therapy medicinal products
metadata.document_version: Final (adopted by CAT February 2018, by CHMP March 2018)
metadata.issuing_body: European Medicines Agency / Committee for Advanced Therapies (CAT)
metadata.replaces: CPMP/BWP/3088/99 (Note for guidance on quality, non-clinical and clinical aspects of gene transfer medicinal products, 2001)

rules.rule_text: For any GTMP MAA, the dossier must be divided into a Drug Substance (DS) and a Drug Product (DP) section, even though the manufacturing process may not conform to the traditional drug substance/drug product format.
rules.verbatim_quote: For any GTMP MAA, the dossier must be divided into a Drug Substance (DS) and a Drug Product (DP) sections, even though the manufacturing process for GTMPs may not conform to the traditional drug substance/drug product format.
rules.exceptions: A pragmatic approach can be taken, keeping the DP section short, e.g. consisting only of the formulation step.

rules.rule_text: A full description of the GTMP must be given including the clinical indication, in vivo mode of action, vector design with explanation of the role of individual components, and the therapeutic sequence(s). Diagrams must be used to illustrate as necessary. The therapeutic sequence(s), junction regions, and regulatory elements must be provided.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1 – General Information on the GTMP
rules.verbatim_quote: A full description of the GTMP should be given. The clinical indication for the product and the in vivo mode of action should be stated: in this context an explanation of the design of the vector should be given along with an outline of the role of individual components and the therapeutic sequence(s). Diagrams should be used to illustrate the description as necessary. The therapeutic sequence(s), junction regions and regulatory elements should be provided.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For replication-deficient viral vectors, demonstration of replication incompetence must begin with a clearly documented strategy. The absence of RCV must be tested on the drug substance, intermediates where appropriate, and packaging/producer cell lines. Screening for RCVs must use a suitably sensitive detector cell line and appropriate passage numbers.
rules.verbatim_quote: For replication deficient viral vectors, demonstration of replication incompetence begins with a clearly documented strategy to render the viral vector replication incompetent. The possibility of any recombination events leading to RCV or replication via trans regulation should be discussed. The absence of RCV is then tested on the drug substance, intermediates where appropriate, as well as any packaging/producer cell lines. Screening for RCVs should be in accordance with Pharmacopoeial recommendations, using a suitably sensitive detector cell line and appropriate passage numbers.
rules.exceptions: Based on risk-based approach, applicant can justify the stage of production at which RCV testing will be performed.

rules.rule_text: For all vectors, documentation of the origin, history and biological characteristics of the parental virus or bacterium must be provided. All genetic elements of the GTMP must be described including those aimed at therapy, delivery, safety, control and production, and the rationale for their inclusion given.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all vectors, documentation of the origin, history and biological characteristics of the parental virus or bacterium should be provided. If historic information about the vector origin is limited, an understanding of the potential implications of the gaps in knowledge should be provided, for example via a risk assessment. All the genetic elements of the GTMP should be described including those aimed at therapy, delivery, safety, control and production and the rationale for their inclusion should be given.
rules.exceptions: If historic information is limited, a risk assessment may substitute.

rules.rule_text: For viral vectors, the entire genome must be sequenced at a level comparable to a production batch unless otherwise justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: For viral vectors: elements to be described include, but are not limited to, the virus backbone, transgene and regulatory sequences. The entire genome should be sequenced at a level comparable to a production batch unless otherwise justified.
rules.exceptions: Justification accepted for omission.

rules.rule_text: The use of antibiotic resistance genes in the final GTMP must be avoided if possible and, where not possible, justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of antibiotic resistance genes in final GTMP should be avoided if possible and where not possible, justified.
rules.exceptions: Must be justified where avoidance is not possible.

rules.rule_text: Vectors must be produced from well-characterised bacterial or virus seeds and/or cell banks. Master and working seed/cell banks must be established and subjected to an appropriate quality control strategy. Control of risk of contamination with adventitious agents is essential.
rules.verbatim_quote: Vectors should be produced from well characterised bacterial or virus seeds and/or cell banks. Master and working seed/cell banks should be established, and subjected to an appropriate quality control strategy (see 4.2.2.1). Appropriate control of the risk of contamination with adventitious agents is essential to ensure microbiological safety of the product.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The number of passages between the working seed/cell lot and vector production must not exceed that used for production of the vector used in clinical studies, unless otherwise justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of passages between the working seed/cell lot and vector production should not exceed that used for production of the vector used in clinical studies, unless otherwise justified.
rules.exceptions: Otherwise justified exceptions accepted.

rules.rule_text: An effective purification process must be in place to reduce or eliminate impurities to acceptable levels. For products containing replication-deficient viruses, a test to detect replication competent viruses in supernatant fluids of producing cells and in the viral fraction at appropriate stages of production is essential.
rules.verbatim_quote: An effective purification process should be in place to reduce or eliminate impurities to acceptable levels (see 4.2.3.3)... For products containing replication-deficient viruses, a test to detect replication competent viruses in supernatant fluids of producing cells and in the viral fraction at appropriate stages of production is essential.
rules.exceptions: Absence of purification steps must be robustly justified based on technical considerations, product quality, and clinical safety and efficacy.

rules.rule_text: All starting materials used for manufacture of the active substance must be listed and information on the source, quality and control must be provided. Bacterial/cell/virus seed banks must be established. Genetic stability of the parent material must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: All starting materials used for manufacture of the active substance should be listed and information on the source, quality and control of these materials must be provided. The establishment of bacterial/cell/virus seed or bank(s) is expected for starting materials which are bankable. The source and history of the cells or bacterial or virus seeds used for generation of the respective banks should be described and genetic stability of the parent material demonstrated.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For virus seed banks, control must include: identity (genetic and immunological), virus concentration and infectious titre, virus genome integrity, expression of therapeutic sequences, biological activity, sterility, absence of mycoplasmas and spiroplasmas, absence of adventitious/contaminating virus, and absence of replication competent virus. Complete sequence of the therapeutic and regulatory elements and, where feasible, the complete virus sequence must be confirmed.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2.1 – Starting materials (i) Virus seed banks
rules.verbatim_quote: Control of virus seed banks should include identity (genetic and immunological), virus concentration and infectious titre, virus genome integrity, expression of the therapeutic sequences or the derived product (protein or RNA), sterility (bacterial, and fungal), absence of mycoplasmas and spiroplasmas (in case insect cells are used during virus seed production), absence of adventitious/contaminating virus and absence of replication competent virus (where the product is replication deficient or replication conditional), inter-vial homogeneity and other relevant characteristics of the virus seed bank. Complete sequence of the therapeutic and the regulatory elements and, where feasible, the complete sequence of the virus in the seed bank should be confirmed as part of the characterisation.
rules.exceptions: Absence of mycoplasmas/spiroplasmas required specifically when insect cells are used.

rules.rule_text: Where materials of ruminant origin are used in preparation of master and working seeds or cell banks, compliance with the relevant TSE note for guidance is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where materials of ruminant origin are used in preparation of the master and working seeds or cell banks, compliance with relevant Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathy (TSE) note for guidance is required.
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: EMA TSE Note for Guidance (EMA/410/01 rev.3)

rules.rule_text: Penicillin, all other β-lactam antibiotics, and streptomycin must neither be used during production nor added to the final product, as they are known to provoke sensitivity in certain individuals. This also applies to other toxic reagents such as ethidium bromide.
rules.verbatim_quote: Penicillin, all other β-lactam antibiotics and streptomycin should neither be used during production nor added to the final product as they are known to provoke sensitivity in certain individuals. This would also apply to other toxic reagents such as ethidium bromide.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: An extensive characterisation of the drug substance must be established in terms of genotypic and phenotypic identity, purity, biological potency/therapeutic sequence activity, infectivity/transduction efficiency and suitability for the intended use, unless otherwise justified.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.3 – Characterisation of the drug substance
rules.verbatim_quote: An extensive characterisation of the DS should be established in terms of genotypic and phenotypic identity, purity, biological potency/therapeutic sequence activity, infectivity/transduction efficiency and suitability for the intended use, unless otherwise justified.
rules.exceptions: Justification required if any dimension is omitted.

rules.rule_text: It must be demonstrated that there is no inclusion of known oncogenic/tumourigenic sequences in the vector or therapeutic sequence.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.3.1 – Elucidation of structure and other characteristics
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be demonstrated that there is no inclusion of known oncogenic/tumourigenic sequences. Tests should be included to show integrity and homogeneity of the recombinant viral genome or plasmid and the genetic stability of the vector and therapeutic sequence.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For viral vectors, insertion sites must be determined where appropriate and the potential for insertional mutagenesis established and associated risks fully evaluated.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.3.1 – Elucidation of structure and other characteristics
rules.verbatim_quote: For viral vectors, insertion sites should be determined where appropriate and the potential for insertional mutagenesis established and associated risks fully evaluated.
rules.exceptions: Where appropriate — justification required for omission.
cross_references: EMA/CAT/190186/2012 Reflection paper on insertional mutagenesis

rules.rule_text: The intended biological action of the GTMP must be demonstrated. The in vitro biological activity of all transgene(s) and any other expressed sequences must be determined. Any selectivity claimed for host range and tropism must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: The intended action of regulating, repairing, replacing, adding or deleting a genetic sequence should be demonstrated. The in vitro biological activity of all transgene(s) and any other expressed sequences should be determined... Any selectivity claimed for the host range and tropism of a viral vector or selectivity of delivery of complexed nucleic acid should be demonstrated.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Drug substance specifications must be justified according to ICH Q6B principles and must normally encompass tests for identity, purity, content, activity, sterility, endotoxin level, and mycoplasma. At least one biological potency specification must be established.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.4 – Specifications for the drug substance
rules.verbatim_quote: The criteria for acceptance or rejection of a production batch must be provided. DS specifications should be given and justified according to principles outlined in ICH guideline Q6B... The specifications for the drug substance should normally encompass tests for identity, purity, content, activity, sterility, endotoxin level and mycoplasma... A suitable measure of the potency of the DS should be established. At least one biological potency specification should be established.
rules.exceptions: Departures from Ph.Eur. 5.14 tests must be justified.

rules.rule_text: For replication-deficient viral vectors of the Retroviridae family, absence of replication competence must be demonstrated using a validated assay. Release lot upper limits for RCV must be qualified on the basis of non-clinical and/or clinical data for batches with similar levels.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of vectors which are potentially hazardous to patients' health in their replication competent forms, such as members of the Retroviridae, absence of replication competence should be demonstrated using a validated assay. In other justified cases, it may be acceptable to release vector lots with an upper limit for replication competent vector. In these cases the justification for the limit should include qualification on the basis of non-clinical and/or clinical data for batches with similar levels.
rules.exceptions: In justified cases, an upper limit may be set with qualification data.

rules.rule_text: If tumourigenic/immortalised cell lines are used during production, the total residual DNA level must be strictly controlled and kept at a minimum unless otherwise justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If tumourigenic / immortalised cell lines are used during production the total residual DNA level should be strictly controlled and kept at a minimum unless otherwise justified.
rules.exceptions: Otherwise justified exceptions accepted.

rules.rule_text: All analytical methods used for release of drug substance and drug product batches must be fully validated according to ICH guidelines and suitable for their purpose. For assays related to toxic impurities and replication competent viruses, the limit of detection must provide assurance of the safety of the vector product.
rules.source_section: Section 4.5 – Analytical Method, Validation and Reference Standards
rules.verbatim_quote: All analytical methods used for release of drug substance and drug product batches should be fully validated according to ICH guidelines and suitable for their purpose. For assays related to impurities which may affect the safety of the product, such as tests for toxic impurities and tests for replication competent viruses, it is essential to establish the suitability and the sensitivity of the tests. The limit of detection must be such that the test provides assurance of the safety of the vector product.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Real-time stability studies must be undertaken for the DS and DP intended for marketing. Vector integrity, biological potency (including transduction capacities) and strength are critical product attributes that must always be included in stability studies.
rules.source_section: Section 4.6 – Stability for drug substance and drug product
rules.verbatim_quote: Real time stability studies should be undertaken, in particular for the DS and DP intended for marketing... Vector integrity, biological potency (including transduction capacities) and strength are critical product attributes which should always be included in stability studies.
rules.exceptions: Accelerated stability studies may provide complementary supporting evidence.

rules.rule_text: Gene therapy vectors other than bacterial vectors are required to be microbiologically sterile. Sterility must be ensured by combination of measures including selection/control of starting materials, exclusion of extraneous material, in-process bioburden controls, bioburden reduction steps, and sterilisation by filtration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Gene therapy vectors other than bacterial vectors are required to be microbiologically sterile. Since it may not be possible to apply direct sterilisation methods such as heat or irradiation, the microbiological sterility of gene therapy vectors should be ensured by the application of a combination of measures including the following: Selection and control of starting material (including seed and cell banks), reagent and excipients and equipment. Exclusion of ingress of extraneous material during the production process. In-process tests and controls focussing on limiting bioburden levels. The application of bioburden reduction process steps, and sterilisation by filtration.
rules.exceptions: Bacterial vectors exempted from this specific requirement.

rules.rule_text: The viral safety of each GTMP must be ensured. Rigorous testing of seed and cell banks, intermediates and end products for the presence of adventitious virus must be conducted in accordance with ICH Q5A(R1). Viral clearance studies must be undertaken where appropriate.
rules.source_section: Section 4.7.2 – Viral and non-conventional adventitious agents
rules.verbatim_quote: The viral safety of each GTMP has to be ensured. Contamination with extraneous viruses and residues of viruses used during production, such as production and helper viruses needs to be excluded as far as possible... Rigorous testing of seed and cell banks, intermediates and end products for the presence of adventitious virus needs to be conducted in accordance with principles outlined in ICH guideline Q5A (R1). Where appropriate, viral clearance studies should be undertaken to determine reduction factors for the relevant step(s) of the production processes.
rules.exceptions: Since virus clearance steps during production are limited for many GTMPs, a combination of measures must be applied.

rules.rule_text: Pivotal non-clinical safety studies must be carried out in conformity with the principles of GLP.
rules.verbatim_quote: Pivotal non-clinical safety studies should be carried out in conformity with the principles of GLP (see: Question and Answer on the Good Laboratory Practices (GLP) in relation to ATMPs).
rules.exceptions: Non-pivotal studies may be conducted without GLP with justification.
cross_references: EMA Q&A on GLP in relation to ATMPs

rules.rule_text: Products used in non-clinical studies must be sufficiently characterised to confirm the studies were conducted with material representative of the product to be administered to humans in clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Products used in non-clinical studies should be sufficiently characterised to provide reassurance that the non-clinical studies have been conducted with material that is representative of the product to be administered to humans in clinical studies.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical studies must be done with the most appropriate pharmacologically relevant in vitro and in vivo models. The rationale for model selection must be discussed and justified. Both genders should generally be used in animal studies or justification given for specific omissions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-clinical studies should be done with the most appropriate pharmacologically relevant in vitro and in vivo models available. The rationale for the non-clinical development and the criteria used to choose these models should be discussed and justified in the non-clinical overview... Both genders should generally be used or justification given for specific omissions.
rules.exceptions: If no appropriate animal model exists, applicant may develop models or perform in vitro evaluations with scientific justification.

rules.rule_text: Proof-of-concept non-clinical studies must be performed to generate evidence supporting the potential clinical effect or related biological effect/molecular mechanism of action, demonstrating that the nucleic acid sequence reaches its intended target and provides its intended function.
rules.source_section: Section 5.3.1 – Primary pharmacodynamic (Proof of concept studies)
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies should generate non-clinical evidence supporting the potential clinical effect or at least provide information on the related biological effect/molecular mechanism of action... In vitro and in vivo studies performed to unravel the mechanism of action relating to the proposed therapeutic use (i.e. pharmacodynamic 'proof of concept' studies) should be performed using relevant animal species and models suitable to show that the nucleic acid sequence reaches its intended target (target organ or cells) and provides its intended function (level of expression and functional activity).
rules.exceptions: In vitro studies acceptable when relevant in vivo disease models are not available.

rules.rule_text: Biodistribution studies must use dosing that mimics clinical use with appropriate margins (e.g. 10-fold the clinical dose adjusted to the animal model). All relevant organs and tissues must be harvested and investigated for presence and clearance of the administered GTMP. Observation period must continue until no signal is detected or a long-term signal plateau is reached.
rules.verbatim_quote: The dosing used for biodistribution studies should mimic the clinical use with appropriate margins, e.g., 10-fold the clinical dose adjusted to the characteristics of the animal model used. The route of administration and the treatment regimen (frequency and duration) should be representative for the clinical use... The observation period of the study should continue until there is no signal detection or until a long-term signal plateau phase is reached. All relevant organs and tissues should be harvested and investigated for presence and clearance of the administered GTMP.
rules.exceptions: Existing biodistribution data from same vector with a different transgene may be taken into account.

rules.rule_text: For integrating vectors, integration studies must focus on: tissues/organs where integration takes place, copy number and localisation of integrated vector copies, structural integrity of the integrated vector, genomic stability of the integrated vector over time, and on-target/off-target genomic integration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Integration studies should focus, at least, on the following issues, unless justified: Tissues/organs where the integration takes place... Copy number and localisation of the integrated vector copies in the host genome... Structural integrity of the integrated vector (in particular the transgene expression cassette of interest), to detect rearrangements/recombination events. Genomic stability of the integrated vector over time and persistency of the average vector copy number in the cells. On-target / off-target genomic integration and the likelihood of off-target integration in case targeted integration is anticipated.
rules.exceptions: A risk-based approach may be used; approach must be justified.

rules.rule_text: The risk of germline transmission must be investigated by biodistribution studies (signal in gonads, gametes, semen fractionation, integration analysis) unless otherwise justified (e.g. clinical indication and/or patient population indicate studies are not warranted).
rules.source_section: Section 5.4.1 – Biodistribution studies (Risk of germline transmission)
rules.verbatim_quote: Administration of certain GTMPs to patients/subjects raises the possibility of vertical germline transmission of vector DNA, which needs to be investigated, unless otherwise justified, e.g. if the clinical indication and / or patient population indicate that such studies are not warranted. The risk for germ line transmission should be addressed primarily at the biodistribution level (signal in gonads, signal in gametes, semen fractionation studies and integration analysis) according to the guideline on non-clinical testing for inadvertent germline transmission of gene transfer vectors.
rules.exceptions: May be omitted with justification based on clinical indication or patient population.
cross_references: EMA/EMEA/273974/2005 – Guideline on non-clinical testing for inadvertent germline transmission

rules.rule_text: Shedding must be addressed in animal models to determine the secretion/excretion profile of the virus/vector, and shedding studies should be integrated into biodistribution or other non-clinical studies where feasible.
rules.verbatim_quote: Shedding is defined as the dissemination of vector through secretions and/or excreta and should be addressed in animal models. While shedding should not be confused with biodistribution (i.e. spread within the body from the site of administration), it is advised to integrate shedding studies into the design of biodistribution studies or other non-clinical studies, when feasible. The aim of shedding studies is to determine the secretion/excretion profile of the virus/vector.
rules.exceptions: If the shedding pattern is already known, additional non-clinical evaluation may not be needed.
cross_references: ICH considerations on general principles to address virus and vector shedding (CHMP/ICH/449035/09)

rules.rule_text: Toxicity must be assessed for the whole GTMP (virus/vector particle/delivery system, nucleic acid sequences) and for the transgene product(s) to determine unwanted consequences of distribution, persistence, infection/transduction/transfection, and expression of the therapeutic gene(s). The possibility of re-assortment and/or recombination with wild type pathogens must be taken into consideration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Toxicity should be assessed for the whole GTMP (virus/vector particle/delivery system, nucleic acid sequences, etc.) and for the transgene product(s) in order to determine unwanted consequences of the distribution and persistence of the vector, its infection/transduction/transfection, the expression and biological activity of the therapeutic gene(s) and vector genes, if applicable, as well as immunogenicity or unwanted pharmacological effects... The possibility of re-assortment and/or recombination with wild type pathogens should be taken into consideration.
rules.exceptions: Extent of non-clinical safety assessment is based on risk-based approach.

rules.rule_text: For GTMPs intended for single administration, single dose toxicology studies with an appropriately extended post-dose observation period must be performed. Single dose toxicity studies must NOT be designed as acute toxicity studies with an endpoint of lethality.
rules.verbatim_quote: For GTMPs intended for single administration, single dose toxicology studies with an appropriately extended post-dose observation period should be performed... Single dose toxicity studies for GTMPs should not be designed as acute toxicity studies with an endpoint of lethality.
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: EMA Guideline on repeated dose toxicity

rules.rule_text: Repeat-dose toxicity studies must be provided when multiple dosing of human subjects is intended. The mode and schedule of administration must reflect clinical dosing. If single dosing results in prolonged function of the nucleic acid sequence in humans but not in the animal model, repeated dose toxicity studies must be considered to mimic the human situation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Repeated-dose toxicity studies should be provided when multiple dosing of human subjects is intended. The mode and schedule of administration should appropriately reflect the clinical dosing. For those cases where single dosing may result in prolonged function of the nucleic acid sequence and/or its product in humans, but not in the animal model, or in case replication kinetics of replicating vectors in animals are not reflecting the situation in humans, repeated dose toxicity studies should be considered to mimic the human situation.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Genotoxicity studies must be addressed using a 3-step approach: (1) investigate genomic modifications and detect abnormal cell behaviour, (2) evaluate toxicity due to insertional mutagenesis, (3) identify/characterise genomic integration sites. Insertional mutagenesis and consequent tumourigenicity must be evaluated in relevant in vitro/in vivo models.
rules.verbatim_quote: Genotoxicity studies might be required depending on the nature of the GTMP. The objectives of such studies can be addressed by a 3 step approach as follows: 1) To investigate occurrences of genomic modification and detect any subsequent abnormal cell behaviour; 2) To evaluate toxicity issues due to insertional mutagenesis and investigate the mechanism driving these adverse toxicity effects... 3) To identify/characterise genomic integration sites (IS) and evaluate possible cross-talk between the transgenic and neighbouring sequences. Genotoxicity issues, including insertional mutagenesis and consequent tumourigenicity should be evaluated carefully in relevant in vitro/in vivo models.
rules.exceptions: Bacteriophages and genetically modified microorganisms (e.g. Lactobacillus, Salmonella) may be considered out of scope for genotoxicity studies.

rules.rule_text: For GTMPs with host-DNA integrative capacity, the potential for integration must be investigated and discussed both where intended and where integration is not intended. Extended in vitro and in vivo assays addressing insertional oncogenesis may be warranted before first administration in humans.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potential for integration of the transgene expression cassette into the host genome should be investigated and discussed both where it is intended and inherent to the method of expression (e.g. when retroviral/lentiviral vectors are used) and in cases where integration is not intended (e.g. when adenoviral, adeno-associated viral or plasmid vectors are used)... Depending on the nature of the vector used, extended in vitro and in vivo assays addressing insertional oncogenesis may be warranted before first administration in human.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Embryo-foetal and perinatal toxicity studies and germline transmission studies must be provided unless otherwise duly justified on the basis of the type of product concerned. Similarly, these studies may be required if women of child-bearing potential are to be exposed to GTMPs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Embryo-foetal and perinatal toxicity studies and germline transmission studies should be provided, unless otherwise duly justified on the basis of the type of product concerned. Similarly, embryo-foetal and perinatal toxicity studies may be required if women of child-bearing potential are to be exposed to GTMPs, depending on the clinical use and clinical population in order to investigate the effect on the foetus such as placental transfer of cytokines produced locally.
rules.exceptions: May be omitted with justification based on product type.
cross_references: EMA guideline on non-clinical testing for inadvertent germline transmission

rules.rule_text: The immune status of the patient (immunocompromised or immunocompetent) and pre-existing immunity against the vector must be determined before treatment with a GTMP.
rules.verbatim_quote: The immune status of the patient i.e. immunocompromised or immunocompetent, as well as pre-existing immunity against the vector should be determined before treatment.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Long-term monitoring of patients treated with a GTMP is required per Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007. Long-term studies must be appropriately designed with sampling plan, sample treatment, analytical methods, and endpoints to maximise information output.
rules.verbatim_quote: Long term monitoring of patients treated with a GTMP is of particular importance, given also the legal requirement of long term efficacy and safety follow up (according to Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007). Those long-term studies should be appropriately designed (e.g. sampling plan, sample treatment, analytical methods, endpoints) in order to maximise information output especially when invasive methods are used.
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: EMEA/CHMP/GTWP/60436/2007 – Guideline on follow-up of patients administered with GTMPs

rules.rule_text: Clinical shedding studies to address the excretion of the GTMP must be performed. When shedding is observed, the potential for transmission to third parties must be investigated, or justification for not doing so must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Shedding studies to address the excretion of the GTMP should be performed. When shedding is observed, the potential for transmission to third parties needs to be investigated, if relevant (e.g. with replication competent vectors/oncolytic viruses) or a justification for not doing this should be provided.
rules.exceptions: Justification for not investigating transmission potential is required if shedding is observed.
cross_references: ICH considerations on general principles to address virus and vector shedding (CHMP/ICH/449035/09)
EMA guideline on environmental risk assessment

rules.rule_text: When there is a risk of shedding through seminal fluid, at least two means of contraception including barrier contraception must be recommended beyond one cycle of spermatogenesis after the last positive sperm sample.
rules.verbatim_quote: Apart from contraceptive measures requested for clinical trials (CTFG Recommendations related to contraception and pregnancy testing in clinical trials), when there is a risk of shedding through the seminal fluid, at least two means of contraception – including barrier contraception should be recommended beyond one cycle of spermatogenesis after the last positive sperm sample.
rules.exceptions: None stated.
cross_references: CTFG Recommendations related to contraception and pregnancy testing in clinical trials

rules.rule_text: A comprehensive evaluation of the immune response to the vector and the transgene product must be performed as part of clinical development. This must include evaluation of cellular and humoral immunity, antibody titre and avidity, whether antibodies are neutralising, and correlation of immunogenicity results with safety and efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: A comprehensive evaluation of the immune response to the vector and the transgene product has to be performed. This might include the evaluation of the cellular and humoral immunity to the vector as well as to the transgene product (e.g. titre and avidity of antibodies and information on whether the antibodies are neutralising or not). The results should be documented in relation to the timing of the treatments and correlation of the immunogenicity results with concurrent safety and efficacy should be provided.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: A safety database must be set up including adverse events linked to the transgene product and/or to the vector or transduction mechanism. Particular attention must be paid to: infusion-related reactions and cytokine release, infection and inflammatory responses, immune-mediated adverse effects, over-expression, malignancy, and any unintended transduction of tissues.
rules.verbatim_quote: A safety database should be set up including any adverse events which are linked to the transgene product and/or to the vector or the transduction mechanism. Risks of the administration procedure, e.g. invasive procedures to administer the GTMP... should be addressed. Particular attention should be paid to: Infusion-related reactions and cytokine release... Infection and inflammatory responses... Immune mediated adverse effects... Over-expression... Malignancy... Any unintended transduction of tissues.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: In clinical trials, samples of patients' sera and peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) taken prior to treatment and at dedicated time points after treatment must be stored to allow investigation of potential for human infection with any adventitious agent transmitted by the GTMP. Storage must comply with current rules/guidelines on biobanking.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the conduct of clinical trials, samples of patients' sera and peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) taken prior to treatment and at dedicated time points after treatment should be stored in order to allow for investigation of the potential for human infection with any adventitious agent transmitted by the GTMP. The duration of storage is depending on patient population/disease, GTMP being administered and the integrity of the stored materials. Consent forms should be prepared and samples storage should be carried out according current rules/guidelines on biobanking.
rules.exceptions: Duration of storage depends on patient population/disease and GTMP.
